nameMeanings.name: Wishart
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names В© Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Scottish (eastern and central Scotland): of Norman origin, from the Anglo-Norman French form, Wischard, of the Old French personal name Guiscard. This was formed in Normandy from the Old Norse elements vizkr вҖҳwiseвҖҷ + hard вҖҳhardy, brave, boldвҖҷ (or possibly the Old French suffix -ard).

variants.name: Wishard
meanings: Scottish: variant of   Wishart  . This surname is very rare in Britain.

variants.name: Wisehart
meanings: Probably an Americanized form of German Weisshardt, from a personal name composed of the ancient Germanic elements wД«s вҖҳwiseвҖҷ + hart вҖҳstrong, hardвҖҷ.
Perhaps an altered form of Scottish   Wishart  .

nameMeanings.name: Baron
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names В© Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: French, English, Dutch, Polish, Czech, Romanian, and Croatian; Breton (also Le Baron): from Old French barun, baron, Middle English barun, baroun, Middle Dutch baroen, Polish, Czech, Romanian, and dialectal Croatian baron вҖҳbaronвҖҷ (i.e. a nobleman ranking below an earl and above a knight), a title of nobility which was in England also awarded to certain freemen of the cities of London and York and of the Cinque Ports. As a surname it is unlikely to be a status name denoting a person of rank. Generally, it referred to service in a baronial household or was acquired as a nickname by a peasant who had ideas above his station or applied to a man showing characteristics of a nobleman. See also 3 and 4 below, compare Barron and Lebaron .
French: habitational name from (Le) Baron, the name of several places in various parts of France. Compare   Lebaron  .
German: status name for a freeman or baron, from barЕ«n вҖҳimperial or church officialвҖҷ, a loanword in Middle High German from Old French (see 1 above).

variants.name: Barron
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Jose, Juan, Jesus, Manuel, Francisco, Carlos, Miguel, Raul, Pedro, Ruben, Alfredo, Luis.

Scottish: status name from Scots baron, which in the Highlands denoted a small proprietary landholder. In Angus it alluded specifically to a tenant of one of the small baronies attached to the Abbey of Coupar Angus. The surname was taken by Scottish migrants to Ulster. Compare Baron .
Scottish: shortened form of McBarron, from Scottish Gaelic Mac a' Bharain вҖҳson of the baronвҖҷ. See Baron and 1 above.

variants.name: Boron
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Polish Krystyna, Edyta, Leszek, Mieczyslaw, Mieczyslawa, Wladyslaw.
Polish (BoroЕ„): from a pet form of the personal names BorzysЕӮaw or Borzymir, formed with the element bor вҖҳto fightвҖҷ (from Old Slavic boriti se).
French: habitational name from a place so called in Franche-ComtГ©.

variants.name: Baro
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Mario, Manuel, Ana, Angel, Carlos, Cesar, Fernando, Jesus, Jose, Orlando, Ramon, Rosalia.  Italian Guido, Angelo, Carmelo, Federico, Lucio.
Catalan (BarГі): status name from barГі вҖҳfree man, baronвҖҷ (see Baron 1), or a habitational name from a place so named.
Hungarian (BГЎrГі): status name from bГЎrГі вҖҳbaronвҖҷ. In the form BarГі, it could be from a pet form of the personal names BarabГЎs (see Barabas ) or Bertalan.

variants.name: Barona
meanings: Spanish: from the feminine form of BarГіn вҖҳbaronвҖҷ (see Baron ), applied as a nickname or as an occupational name for a member of the household of a baroness.
Italian: probably from the feminine form of   Barone  . The name is concentrated in Sondrio province (Lombardy).

variants.name: Barren
meanings: English: probably a variant of   Baron   or   Barran  .

nameMeanings.name: of
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names В© Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Sung, Jung, Yong, Chang, Kwang, Sang, Dong, Hyun, Jae, Jin, Kyung, Jong, Chong, Chung, Byung, Jeong, Pyong, Myong, Hak, Jin Hwan, Sook, Yeong, Choon.
Korean: variant of   O  .
Japanese: variant of ЕҢ (see O ).

variants.name: O
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Young, Bo Young, Chae, Chong Hwan, Dong Kun, Ho, Hwan, Hyun, Jong, Jung, Ki Soon, Kwang.  Korean Min, Chong, Chol, Jung Ho, Myong, Sa, Sung Kyun.

Korean (мҳӨ; Chinese character: еҗі): historically, there were two Chinese characters for the O surname, but by the late KoryЕҸ period (14th century AD ), one of them had disappeared. Some records indicate that there are as many as 210 different clans for the remaining character, but only sixteen can be positively documented. All sixteen of these clans originate from the same ancestor, O ChвҖҷЕҸm (еҗі зһ»), who migrated to Korea from China during the reign of Shilla's King ChijЕӯng (500вҖ“514 AD ). O is a common surname throughout Korea. Compare Oh .

nameMeanings.name: Plenderleith
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of Family Names in Britain and Ireland В© University of the West of England 2016
meanings: Scottish: locative name from Plenderleith in Oxnam (Perths).

nameMeanings.name: John
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names В© Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: As a boy's name this represents a revival of Middle English Jan, a byвҖҗform of John , or an adoption of the common European form with this spelling. As a girl's name it is a short form of names such as Janet and Janice .

en.lifeSummary: Sir John Wishart - Baron of Plenderleith was born in 1260, in Scotland as the son of Andrew Wishart. He had at least 1 son with Johanna de Plenderleith - Baroness of Plenderleith.
